stem: Find the slope of the line through \( ([[x1]],[[y1]]) \) and \( ([[x2]],[[y2]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the slope, using \( \Delta x/\Delta y\) instead of \( \Delta y/\Delta x\).
This adds the coordinates instead of subtracting them.
This flips the sign of the numerator only, mismatching it with the denominator's order.
This flips the sign of the denominator only, mismatching it with the numerator's order.
This is off by one from the correct slope.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct slope.

stem: Find the distance between the points \( ([[x1]],[[y1]]) \) and \( ([[x2]],[[y2]]) \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to take the square root, reporting \( d^{2}\) instead of \( d\).
This uses taxicab (Manhattan) distance instead of Euclidean distance.
This subtracts the squared differences instead of adding them.
This adds the coordinates instead of subtracting them before squaring.
This is off by one from the correct distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct distance.

stem: Find the distance from the point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) to the line \( [[A]]x+[[B]]y+[[C]]=0 \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the absolute value, allowing a negative distance.
This forgets to take the square root in the denominator.
This drops the constant term \( C\) from the numerator.
This multiplies by \( \sqrt{A^2+B^2}\) instead of dividing by it.
This is off by one from the correct distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct distance.

stem: Find the \( x \)-coordinate of the intersection point of the lines \( y=[[m1]]x+[[b1]] \) and \( y=[[m2]]x+[[b2]] \).
solution: Setting the equations equal, \( [[m1]]x+[[b1]]=[[m2]]x+[[b2]] \) gives \( x=\dfrac{b_2-b_1}{m_1-m_2}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the numerator's sign reversed relative to the denominator's, flipping the result.
This adds the intercepts and slopes instead of subtracting them.
This inverts the correct ratio.
This reports the difference of slopes alone, ignoring the intercepts entirely.
This is off by one from the correct intersection coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct intersection coordinate.

stem: A circle has center \( ([[cx]],[[cy]]) \) and radius \( [[r]] \). From the external point \( ([[px]],[[py]]) \), find the length of a tangent segment to the circle.
solution: The tangent segment, the radius to the point of tangency, and the segment from the point to the center form a right triangle, so the tangent length is \( t=\sqrt{d^{2}-r^{2}} \), where \( d \) is the distance from \( ([[px]],[[py]]) \) to the center. This gives \( t=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds \( r^2\) instead of subtracting it, as if using the Pythagorean theorem backwards.
This subtracts \( r\) from the distance directly instead of using the Pythagorean relation on the squares.
This forgets to take the square root, reporting \( t^{2}\) instead of \( t\).
This reports the distance to the center itself, ignoring the radius entirely.
This is off by one from the correct tangent length.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct tangent length.
